# Container topologies across the engines 🟡

**Goal.** Express the same multi-service idea in Docker, Podman, and LXD/Incus — and see what each abstraction buys you.

**Audience:** you can start a container; now wire several together  
**Effort:** ⏱ ~1–2h  
**Prerequisites:** [Containers from first principles](path-container-internals.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`docker-examples/docker-3svc-topology.toml`](../docker-examples/docker-3svc-topology.toml) 🔑 | a rootful, declarative bridge topology. | nginx + postgres + an alpine client share one bridge; the client resolves the others by name. |
| 2 | [`podman-examples/podman-plain-single.toml`](../podman-examples/podman-plain-single.toml) 🪶 | the simplest rootless container with a published port. | `curl localhost:<port>` from the host reaches the container. |
| 3 | [`podman-examples/podman-pod-3svc.toml`](../podman-examples/podman-pod-3svc.toml) 🪶 | a pod shares net/IPC/PID across its containers. | the three containers talk to each other over `localhost`. |
| 4 | [`podman-examples/podman-quadlet-service.toml`](../podman-examples/podman-quadlet-service.toml) 🪶 | a `.container` quadlet becomes a systemd-user unit that survives reboots. | `systemctl --user status` shows the generated unit; it comes back after `systemctl --user restart`. |
| 5 | [`podman-examples/podman-multiarch-build.toml`](../podman-examples/podman-multiarch-build.toml) 🪶 🐌 | build an image for a *foreign* architecture via qemu-user-static. | `file` on the built image's binary reports the other architecture. |
| 6 | [`lxd-examples/lxd-vm-single.toml`](../lxd-examples/lxd-vm-single.toml) | LXD/Incus runs real QEMU VMs under the same API as its containers. | `incus list` shows TYPE=VIRTUAL-MACHINE (needs `/dev/kvm` + block storage). |
| 7 | [`lxd-examples/lxd-mixed-topology.toml`](../lxd-examples/lxd-mixed-topology.toml) | containers and a VM in one lab — the container/VM discriminator. | the lab holds 2 containers + 1 VM in a single `incus list`. |
| 8 | [`lxd-examples/lxd-profiles-projects.toml`](../lxd-examples/lxd-profiles-projects.toml) | profiles bundle config; projects isolate namespaces. | `incus project list` shows the lab's project isolating its instances. |

### Optional side-quests

- [`tiny-internet-project`](../tiny-internet-project/) — capstone — six Debian containers wired into a miniature internet (BIND primary+secondary AXFR, apt-cacher-ng, Postfix+Dovecot, LAMP+phpMyAdmin).
